purpose: Documentation page for the guide area: 2.4 Chat Code Git and Preview. It explains operational behavior, user workflow, data ownership, or architecture details that future agents use when updating Synapse docs or app behavior. Key content: Synapse combines chat, code inspection, git workflows, and app preview so you can keep one loop while agents work. The file is 85 lines long and should be treated as a knowledge/control-plane source rather than application code.
gotchas: Documentation may describe behavior that has moved into Electron IPC/services or .claude skills; verify implementation before making broad user-facing claims.
patterns: Documentation pattern: H1 title, short introduction, then operational sections with headings, tables, examples, or path references for future maintainers.
Uses fenced code blocks for command examples, schemas, or prompt templates.
conventions: Markdown headings use a single H1 followed by H2/H3 sections for navigable documentation.
Guide pages use numeric filename prefixes so the in-app Guide modal can display them in a stable reading order.
Shell examples use fenced `bash` code blocks.
